generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LACHNANTHES. Lachnanthes tinctoria, Ell. Natural order , Hæmodoraceæ. Common name , Red-root. Preparation , Tincture of the whole plant. Authorities. 1 , Dr. Lippe's provings, Am. Hom. Rev., 4, p. 458, W. Raue took 3d dilution (first and second days), 15th dilution (third day), 30th dilution (tenth day); 2 , Mrs. B. took 3d dilution (first day), 15th dilution (second day); 3 , Charles Raue took 3d dilution twice (first and fourth days), one drop of tincture (fifth day); 4 , Miss B. took 6th dilution, one dose of 2 pellets. MIND. ► Lively, singing, and whistling (first day), [1] . ► Towards evening he is in good spirits (third day), [1] . ► Low-spirited (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . ► Ill-humored and sleepy, at 5 P.M. (fifth day), [4] . ► Became much excited over a trifle; he had suppressed his anger, in the evening (second day), [1] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Giddiness in the head (after five minutes, third day), [1] . ► General Head. ► Sensation of dulness in the head (second day), [1] . ► Headache till 10 A.M. (sixteenth day), [4] . ► Headache, with pressing out of the eyes, in the forenoon (fourth day), [1] . [10.] ► Dull headache, [4] . ► Stinging headache every day for about four weeks (after twenty-four days), [4] . ► Headache in the morning, worse at noon; she becomes more giddy; in the evening the headache is a stinging pain; at 6 P.M. the head feels enlarged, and as if split open with a wedge from the outside to within; the body is icy cold, she cannot get warm, although one and a half hours under a feather bed; the whole face becomes yellow; she has to whine with the headache; the head burns like fire, with much thirst (eighth day); same attack, but less severe in the evening (ninth day), [4] . ► Forehead. ► Dull pain in the forehead (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . ► Pain in the forehead (first night), [2] . ► Pain in the right side of the forehead, extending around to the right side of the occiput, when lying down in bed; shortly afterwards a dull pain over the occiput, which was followed by stiffness of the neck (fifth day), [1] . ► Dull headache in the forehead and temples (fifth day), [3] . ► Drawing of the skin on the forehead upwards, worse on the left side (after one hour and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► Pain in the forehead, drawing from the right to the left side, better after eating, towards evening (first day), [3] . ► A continued stitch in the left part of the forehead from within to without (after fifteen minutes); the stinging changed in a few minutes into a pressing pain, and extended from the left part of the forehead to the right side, over the whole forehead, as if it were pressing from within outwards; the pressing pain in the forehead became a dull sensation, in the whole forehead (first day), [1] . [20.] ► Tearing in the forehead, from the left to the right side (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Tearing in the forehead from the left temple to the right, in the evening; the tearing in the forehead continues all night, and awakens him several times from his sleep (sixth day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right side of the forehead down into the cheeks (after three hours and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► Painful tearing in the forehead, in the open air (fifth day), [1] . ► Temples. ► Pressing in the right temple (fifth day), [1] . ► Vertex. ► Sensation as if the vertex was enlarged, and was extended upwards (after fifteen minutes, first day), [1] . ► On the left upper vertex pressing as by a finger (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Tearing in the vertex (after eight hours and a quarter, third day), [1] . ► External Head. ► Sensation as if the hair was standing on end, worse on the occiput (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . ► The whole scalp is painful when touched (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . [30.] ► The scalp is very painful on the vertex (after first dose, fourth day), [3] . ► The scalp is very painful, especially when touched on the vertex (fifth day), [3] . EYE. ► Objective. ► Bright, sparkling eyes (twelfth day), [4] . ► Secretion of white mucus from the eyes, [1] . ► Twitching of the canthus (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Subjective. ► Dryness of the eyes (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Pressing in the left eye, in the afternoon (tenth day), [1] . ► Pressing in both external canthi, in the morning (third day), [1] . ► Pressing in the eyes, as from dust, first in the right and then in the left (after three hours and a half, first day), [1] . ► Pressing as from dust in the eyes, with secretion of white mucus from the eyes, [1] . [40.] ► Pressing out of the eyes, with headache, in the forenoon (fourth day), [1] . ► Pressing pain over the left eye, near the glabella (seventh day), [1] . ► The right eye begins to itch, but does not become red (sixth day), [3] . ► Itching in the left internal canthus of the eye (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Brow. ► Drawing of the eyebrows and eyelids upwards, so that he looks with fixed eyes; even when he tries to bring them down with force, they continually go up again; this sensation lasts one hour (after three-quarters of an hour, second day), [1] . ► Lids. ► The margin of the eyelids a little swollen, in the afternoon (fourth day), [1] . ► When closing the eyes, visible twitching of the upper eyelids; closing them firmly makes it worse (fifth day), [3] . ► Sensation of twitching in the upper eyelids (after three hours and a half, first day), [1] . ► The left upper eyelid, near the inner canthus, begins to itch in the afternoon; a small hard knot is formed on the spot, which becomes red (fifth day); the redness has extended farther, and is covered by little vesicles; they itch a great deal (sixth day); it has almost disappeared (tenth day), [3] . ► Lachrymal Apparatus. ► Sensation in the left os lachrymal, as if something separated itself, afterwards extending from that part over the left eye into the left temple; behind the left ear a bruised pain (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Ball. [50.] ► Sensation as if the eyeball was pressed together from below (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . ► Pupil. ► Pupils enlarged (after five minutes, third day), [1] . ► Pupils very much enlarged (after three-quarters of an hour, second day), [1] . ► Vision. ► Dimness of sight (after half an hour, tenth day), [1] . ► Obscuration of sight, lasting from one to two hours (second day), [1] . ► If he moved his head quickly, his sight became quite obscure (after five minutes, third day), [1] . ► Sight became obscured (after five minutes); disappearing while walking about, returning after sitting down (after fifteen minutes, third day), [1] . ► Sight became obscure; if he looked intently on anything he saw gray rings, which were fixed to the spot (after fifteen minutes, first day), [1] . ► If he read any time, all became black before his eyes, and he had to look away (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► If he looked at any spot for some time, it became quite dark (second day), [1] . [60.] ► While reading, a yellow spot on the paper as large as a hand, which follows as he reads further on, in the forenoon (fifth day), [1] . ► When reading MS ., a small gray spot as large as a lentil is running before the left eye, but not when reading print or when sewing, from 2 to 5 P.M. (fifth day); also (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . EAR. ► Tearing in the right ear (seventh day), [1] . ► Tearing in the left ear (fourth and seventh days), [1] . ► Tearing in the left ear, of short duration (after three hours and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► While eating, crawling in the right ear (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Tingling in the right ear (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . ► Tingling in the left ear, relieved by boring with the finger, but it begins again immediately, and then the ear feels as if closed, with singing in it (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Itching in the left ear, and soon afterwards in the right ear, towards evening (first day), [3] . ► Itching in the right ear; after introducing the finger and taking it out again, sensation as if cold air was rushing into the ear (fourth day), [2] . ► Hearing. [70.] ► Singing before the left ear (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . ► Singing in the right ear, while walking in the open air, in the afternoon (sixth day), [1] . ► Cracking in the right ear, followed by a sensation as if the ear would discharge (fourth day), [3] . NOSE. ► Objective. ► Coryza after the cough and sore throat, at night in bed (third night), [4] . ► Violent bleeding of the nose (half a pint); the blood is pale (sixth day); bleeding of the nose, but less violent, at 4 P.M. (seventh day), [4] . ► Subjective. ► Burning on the right side of the root of the nose (eighth day), [1] . ► A pressure as from a thumb on the right side of the nose (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . [The prover had fallen when a child and broken the nasal bones, which had partly suppurated and left an indentation.] ► A strong pressure on the left side of the nose (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Crawling in the left nostril (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . FACE. ► The whole face becomes yellow (eighth day), [4] . [80.] ► Circumscribed redness of the face, in the afternoon (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), at 8 P.M., [1] . ► Pressing tearing in the left cheek, towards the eye, as if the eye would be pressed out (third day), [1] . MOUTH. ► Sensation as if the upper eye teeth and incisors were loosened, with sensation of soreness, worse when closing the teeth or when touching them with the tongue (after eight hours and a quarter, third day), [1] . ► Pain in all the teeth whilst drinking coffee (after three hours and a half, first day), [1] . ► Pain in all the teeth; they feel too long and loose, in the afternoon; toothache worse in bed (twentieth, twenty-first, and twenty-second days), [4] . ► Sudden pain in all the teeth, while eating broth at noon; pain in all the upper teeth after eating (sixth day), [1] . ► Tearing from the left temple down to the left molar teeth (after one hour, second day), [1] . ► On the left side of the tongue, far back, sensation as from biting on it (second day), [1] . ► Saliva thick and tough (after three hours and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► Tincture tasted bitter (fifth day), [1] . THROAT. [90.] ► Scraping throat, with stinging when swallowing, sleeplessness, dryness increasing, afterwards hoarseness, at 10 P.M. (second day); throat is better, hoarseness continues till 2 P.M. followed by burning and itching on the right knee, which looks scarlet-red (third day), [4] . ► Sensation of swelling in the pharynx; an itching sensation there when swallowing (three hours and a half, first day), [1] . STOMACH. ► Aversion to meat (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Much first, [4] . ► Sweetish rising of water, with nausea, at 4 P.M. (fourteenth day), [4] . ► Hiccough in bed, lasting half an hour (tenth day), [1] . ► Sudden qualmishness in the stomach (second day), [1] . ► When walking in the open air, sensation of squeamishness above the navel (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Rumbling in the left side of the stomach, very soon; later in the left side of the abdomen, then as if it were in the large intestines low down; can hear it, but does not feel it (first day), [2] . ► Fulness in pit of stomach (fifth day), [3] . ABDOMEN. [100.] ► In the upper part of the abdomen, one inch above the navel, turning and twirling (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Cutting in the right upper side of the abdomen (seventh day), [1] . ► Cutting pain in the right side of the abdomen over the navel (after seven hours, third day), [3] . ► Rolling of wind in the abdomen (fifth day), [2] . ► Much rumbling in the abdomen (first night), [2] . ► Frequent rumbling and rolling in the abdomen (fourth day), [2] . ► Continuous rumbling and rolling in the abdomen (after three hours, second day), [2] . ► Much rumbling and rolling in the abdomen and discharge of flatulency (fifth day), [3] . ► Sensation of heat in the abdomen, with a sensation as if the bowels would be moved, soon; after a movement of bowels, the sensation decreased (first day), [2] . ► Cutting in the upper part of the abdomen from the left to the right side (first day), [2] . [110.] ► Sensation as if the bowels would be moved, but they were not (after each dose, fourth day), [3] . ► Sensation in the left groin as if wind was moving in it (after seven hours and a half, second day), [1] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► A continuous stitch in the anus, in the forenoon (first day), [1] . ► Slight inclination to stool (fifth day), [3] . ► Frequent inclination to stool without success (third and fourth days), [1] . STOOL. ► Bowels are much moved, evacuation natural, passes much wind and has to strain a great deal, at 5.45 P.M. (after a quarter of an hour); has usually, one regular evacuation in the morning, nose in the afternoon (first day), [2] . ► Bowels moved twice, morning and night (second day); twice in the morning (third day), [2] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Pressing in the bladder during micturition (after two hours and a half, first day), [1] . ► Some drops flow out of the urethra, coloring the shirt red, during the night (tenth day), [1] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Strong burning in the left half of the scrotum, extending towards the right side (after two hours, second day), [1] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. [120.] ► Burning in the right side of the larynx (fourth day), [1] . ► Short cough, with sore throat, at night in bed (third day); worse in bed and preventing sleep (fourth and fifth days); slight (sixth day); a little, in morning (ninth day); some, in evening (tenth day), [4] . CHEST. ► Feels full in the chest, and this compels him to take a long breath (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . ► Felt hot in the chest and around the heart (after five minutes, third day), [1] . ► Awakens at 2 A.M. with a crampy pain in the chest extending from the right to the left side; afterwards pain in the forehead, with general heat alternating with the above-mentioned pain in the chest; at last it moves into the neck and shoulders (first night), [2] . ► Stitches in the left side of the chest (seventh day), [1] . ► Stitches as with a knife in quick succession, in the right side of the chest under the right breast; motion or rest has no influence; afterwards stitches as with knives in the region of the left collar-bone, after midnight (second night); again under the right breast, at 8 A.M. (third day), [2] . ► Boiling and bubbling in the chest and the region of the heart; it ascends up to the head and makes him quite giddy; breaks out into perspiration (after three-quarters of an hour, second day), [1] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Sensation of heat in the region of the heart, going and coming (after fifteen minutes, third day), [1] . ► When lying, he feels the beating of his heart to his head (after three hours and three-quarters, third day), [1] . [130.] ► Pulse 54, at 5 P.M. (fourth day), [1] . ► Pulse 58 (after six hours, first day); 60 (after one hour and a quarter, second day); 64, in the morning (third day), [1] . ► Pulse 60 (after three-quarters of an hour); 74, some beats are slow, others are fast (after one hour and three-quarters); 54 (after three hours and three-quarters); 68 (after six hours and three-quarters); 76 (after twelve hours and a quarter, third day), [1] . ► Pulse 84 (before taking); 80 (after twenty minutes); 72, after lying down and sleeping (two hours and twenty minutes after first dose, first day); 74 (on taking second dose); 58 (four hours after second dose, fourth day); 86 (fifth day), [3] . NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► *Stiffness of the neck (fifth day), [1] . ► Pain in the neck and shoulders (first night), [2] . ► Pain and stiffness in the neck, going over the whole head down to the nose, then like pinching the nostrils together (after one hour, second day), [1] . ► Pain in the nape of the neck, as if from dislocation, when turning the neck or bending the head backward (after two hours, second day), [1] . ► Back. ► Burning in the spine, about four inches above the small of the back (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Sensation of pressure under the short ribs of the right side of the back, deepseated, while taking a long breath (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . ► Dorsal. [140.] ► Burning on the right shoulder-blade (eighth day), [1] . ► Stitches as from the stings of bees on the inner edge of the shoulder-blade (after two hours and a half, first day), [1] . ► Lumbar. ► Burning in the region of the left kidney, deep internally, drawing towards the right side, soon after the burning in the juncture of the ilium with the sacrum (after three-quarters of an hour, second day), [1] . ► Sacral. ► Burning in the os sacrum (after seven hours and a half, second day), [1] . ► Burning in the right side above the sacrum (fourth day), [1] . ► Burning in the spine four inches above the sacrum, in the forenoon (fourth and seventh days), [1] . ► Burning in the juncture of the ilium with the sacrum, deep internally, drawing towards the right (after three-quarters of an hour); soon after burning in the region of the left kidney, deep internally, drawing towards the right side (second day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right ischium (second day); deep inside (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Tearing in the limbs (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Tearing in the left upper arm from the elbow-joint to the shoulder-joint; it pains most in the elbow-joint (after five hours and a half, first day), [1] . [150.] ► Tearing in both elbow-joints, sometimes upwards, then downwards, frequently through the day (seventh day), [1] . ► Tearing in the left elbow-joint (after one hour, second day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right arm below the elbow (eighth day), [1] . ► It drew the left index finger crooked (after one hour and a half, first day), [1] . ► Tearing in the knuckles of the middle fingers of the right hand (tenth day), [1] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Thigh. ► Burning on the right thigh, far up on the inside (after three-quarters of an hour, third day), [1] . ► Knee. ► Pain below the left knee-pan (seventh day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right knee, while walking (tenth day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right knee-cap (ninth day), [1] . ► Tearing in the left knee (tenth day), [1] . ► Leg. [160.] ► Tingling in both lower legs and feet, through and through, but worse in the calf (second day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right tibia (eighth day); in the afternoon (fourth day), [1] . ► Cramp (spasm) in the right calf, in the morning when lying in bed (fourth day), [1] . ► Foot. ► While running, suddenly a sensation as if the left foot was out of joint, so that he stumbles, afterwards tearing in the joint of the left foot (fourth day), [1] . ► Stitch behind the right external malleolus (after three hours, first day), [2] . ► Cramp in the left foot, continuing for half an hour, about 9 A.M. (sixth day), [4] . ► Cramp in the left foot, at night (seventeenth night), [4] . ► Toes. ► Burning in the left big toe (after one hour and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► Burning under the nail of the left toe (after one hour, first day), [1] . ► Tearing in the right big toe, waking him from his sleep (sixth night), [1] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. [170.] ► Feels weak (ninth day), [4] . ► Restless, throws herself about (first night), [3] . ► Very restless while perspiring (eleventh night), [4] . SKIN. ► Objective. ► Appearance of some few little pimples in the bends of the elbows (fifth day), [3] . ► On the left gluteus muscle just on the point is formed a pimple with hard red circumference of the size of a penny, is very painful, when touched exceedingly sore, afterwards burning (sixth day); the pimple does not break, but is very painful (tenth day); a few more pimples formed around the gluteus muscle, they itch, and when opened by scratching ooze out some watery substance (eleventh day), [3] . ► On the forehead elevated red pimples (fifteenth day); they become larger and suppurate (sixteenth day), [4] . ► Subjective. ► Itching and burning of the skin, lasting all night, worse after scratching (third night), [4] . ► Stinging-itching all over, for one hour (thirteenth day), [4] . ► Sensation on the skin of the inside of the left lower leg as if blisters were bursting; soon after the same sensation on the right lower limb (after fifteen minutes, first day), [1] . ► Burning and itching on the right knee, which looks scarlet-red (third day), [4] . [180.] ► Burning-stinging in the left knee-pan of short duration (after seven hours, third day), [1] . ► Burning-stinging pain over the left knee-pan (after three hours); above the left knee-pan, on a spot the size of a cent (after nine hours and a half, second day), [2] . ► Tingling in the lower extremities (after fifteen minutes, third day), [1] . ► Tingling in the lower parts of both legs from the knees to the ankles (first day), [1] . ► Tingling and itching about the scrotum and around it (after three hours and three-quarters, third day), [1] . ► Itching as if deep within the right thigh (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Itching in the left tibia (after one hour and a half, second day), [1] . ► Crawling-itching on the left elbow-joint, soon afterwards on the right elbow-joint, continues for several days, and is worse in the evenings (fifth day), [9] . ► Stinging-itching on the lower side of the first joint of the right second toe, when lying down in the afternoon (fourth day), [3] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► Drowsiness during the day (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Sleeplessness. [190.] ► Cannot go to sleep, early, contrary to his habit (fifth day), [1] . ► Awakens often during the night, contrary to his habit (second night), [1] . ► During the night she is over-wakeful without much difficulty (symptoms?), (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Restless sleep at night, followed by perspiration (first night), [4] . ► Little and restless sleep, during the night (third night), [4] . ► No sleep before midnight (eleventh night), [4] . ► No sleep till morning, when she has feverish distressing dreams (eighteenth night), [4] . ► Sleepless (first eighteen nights, except sixteenth night, and twentieth, twenty-first, and twenty-second nights), [4] . ► Dreams. ► Distressing dreams every night (fifth day), [4] . ► Dreams that she is spinning, and marked her yarn with red wool (first night), [2] . [200.] ► When sleeping, she sees all kinds of images (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. ► Body icy cold, she cannot get warm, although one hour and a half under a feather-bed; head burns like fire, with much thirst (during headache); (eighth and ninth days), [4] . ► Heat. ► She feels hot and oppressed in chest, with mild perspiration all over, of short duration (after four hours and a half, first day), [2] . ► General heat alternating with crampy pain in the chest (first night), [3] . ► Burning heat, with redness of the face, more on the right side; after the heat had ceased circumscribed dark redness of the cheeks , worse on the right side, in the afternoon (twelfth days), [4] . ► Dry heat; the feet burn, she is restless, throws herself about; much rumbling in the abdomen (first night), [2] . ► Flushes of heat alternating with chilliness (nineteenth day), [4] . ► Feverish at 8 P.M. (nineteenth day), [4] . ► Fever from 6 to 12 P.M., then better, with redness of the face, and more on the upper part of the face (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Burning in the palms of the hands and soles of the feet (twenty-fourth and twenty-fifth days), [4] . ► Sweat. [210.] ► Breaks out into perspiration (after three-quarters of an hour, second day), [1] . ► Perspiration, following restless sleep (first night), [4] . ► Perspiration and itching of the penis and scrotum, for some days (fifth day), [1] . ► Much perspiration after midnight (eleventh night); continued perspiration, in the morning (twelfth day), [4] . ► During the cold sensation, the skin is moist and sticky (eighth and ninth days), [4] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Pressing in canthi. ► ( Forenoon ), Headache, etc.; pressing out of the eyes, etc. ► ( Afternoon ), At 5 o'clock, ill-humored, etc.; pressing in left eye; from 2 to 5 o'clock, when reading MS., spot before eyes; while walking in open air, singing in ear; redness of face. ► ( Evening ), Itching in elbow-joint. ► ( Night ), From 6 to 12 o'clock, fever; after midnight, perspiration. ► ( Open air ), Tearing in forehead. ► ( In bed ), Toothache. ► ( While drinking coffee ), Pain in teeth. ► ( Eating ), Pain in teeth. ► ( Walking ), Tearing in knee. ► Amelioration. ► ( Towards evening ), In good spirits. ► ( After eating ), Pain in forehead. ► ( Walking about ), All symptoms; obscuration of sight. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
Clinical: Cough; Diphtheria; Headache; Heart, heat in; pains in; Phthisis; Pneumonia, typhoid; Sore throat; Stiff-neck; Wry-neck

Burning in r. side of larynx. Hoarseness (forenoon). Cough dry, as if it came from larynx (with sore throat); expectoration streaked with blood, with severe pain in chest (pneumonia). Sensation of fulness in chest; is compelled to inhale deeply. Stitches in l. side of chest. Stitches (like knives) under r. breast and under clavicle. Cough in bed and after sleeping. Feels hot and oppressed in chest, with gentle perspiration all over. Tearing from the shoulder, extending to finger-joints. Thumb and index-finger feel as if sprained. Tearing in elbow-joints. Tearing in knuckles of the middle fingers of the r. hand. Burning of palms and soles. Tearing in r. ischium. Small pimples around l. gluteus muscle, which discharge a watery fluid when scratched open. Burning stinging in, above, or below the knee-pan. Tearing in l. knee. Tingling in both lower limbs and feet, in heat. Burning in feet (soles). Cramps in the feet (during night). Very restless; throws herself about; feels weak.

keynotes.text: Lachnan. is a herb, growing in sandy swamps near the coast. The root is red and fibrous; leaves sword-shaped; stem hairy; flowers yellow, woolly. It belongs to the same order as Aletris, and is allied to the Irises. The head, the chest, and the circulation seem most prominently affected by Lachn. There are headaches of great intensity; right-sided, as if split open with a wedge; with icy coldness of body; crying spells; by vomiting. In the chest there are lancinating stitches, in quick succession in right side of chest below mamma. Stitches in heart with anxiety. A peculiar symptom is: Sensation of heat, bubbling and boiling in chest and region of heart, rising to head; giddy; breaks out into perspiration. Trembling of heart. There is also burning of palms and soles and burning under nail of left toe. On the other hand, there is much coldness and chilliness. Continued chilliness. Sensation as if a piece of ice was lying on back between shoulders, then a shock followed by coldness over whole body with gooseflesh. These attacks occur on moving, > after going to bed. Cannot get warm under a feather bed. A hot flat-iron > icy coldness of body. Lachn. has been largely used in cases of phthisis, often empirically, but sometimes on its indications. Arthur Dalzell reports these cases (H. W., xxv. 158): (1) Mrs. B., 43, fibroid phthisis of both lungs, collapse of right lower lobe. Great prostration; night-sweats; bowels alternately relaxed and confined; pain and stiffness in back; bruised all over; dry cough; roughness of throat, heart-sounds weak. Sleep bad owing to cough. Lachn. Ø, three drops every four hours. In three days the prostration was diminished, and the cough was much better, night-sweats and bruised feeling gone. From this time an emulsion containing cod-liver oil, liquor pancreaticus, eggs, cream and hypophosphites, and oil of Eucalyptus were given in addition. The case went on to recovery. (2) Mrs. R., 45, in last stage of consumption. Right lung consolidated; left in cavities. Remittent fever. This patient had circumscribed flush on face; brilliant eyes; throat rough; felt bruised all over. Skin dry, except at night, when she had profuse perspiration. Constant dry cough. Muco-purulent expectoration. Lachn. Ø, five drops every three hours, relieved all the symptoms and procured euthanasia, the patient dying quite easily twelve days later.─Toothache is eating and drinking warm things. With fever there is loquacious delirium, brilliant eyes, and circumscribed red cheeks (there is also a blueness about face, blue under eyes, blue lips). During the cold sensation the skin is damp and clammy. The loquacity of Lachn. is remarkable. Hering says the Indians chew the plant when they meet the white man to give them a flow of language. Lachn. has been used with success in diphtheria, typhoid fever, typhoid pneumonia. A peculiar symptom is stiffness of the neck, the head being twisted to one (right) side. This occurring in connection with sore throat is a strong indication. There is much dryness of throat, which is on waking in the night, with much coughing. Sensations are: As if head enlarged; as if split open with a wedge; vertex as if enlarged; as if hair standing on end. As if a cloud before eyes; pressing as from dust in eyes. As if something had closed ear. Bridge of nose as if pinched (confirmed by E. E. Case─H. P., xiii. 76). As of something crawling over face. A curious observation is quoted in Brit. J. of H. (xxxix. 181), that the while pigs and sheep of Virginia lose their hoofs if they feed on Lachn., while the black varieties eat it without inconvenience.

A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. LACHNANTHES (Lachnanthes Tinctoria.) Natural order.- Haemodoraceae. Common name.- Red Root. General Analysis.- Acts upon the cerebro-spinal system, affecting particularly the sensorium and muscular system, its tendency being to the production of an active congestion and inflammation. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Lachnanthes) Became much excited over a trifle. Delirium with brilliant eyes. (Bell., Stram.) Head.- (Lachnanthes) Dull headache, particularly frontal. Tearing in forehead from left to right side. Scalp painful to the touch. Vertex feels enlarged and extended upward. Eyes.- (Lachnanthes) Yellow spots before the eyes. Bright, sparkling eyes with delirium. (Bell., Stram.) Pressing as from dust in the eyes, with secretion of white mucus. Twitching of upper eyelids (Agar.) ; worse on closing them tightly. Sight became obscured. Ears.- (Lachnanthes) Itching or tingling in ears ; relieved by boring with the finger, but again returns. Singing in the ear. Face.- (Lachnanthes) Circumscribed redness of the face (Sang.), with delirium and brilliant eyes. (Bell.) Mouth.- (Lachnanthes) Pain in all the teeth, aggravated by warmth. Throat.- (Lachnanthes) Sore throat, with short cough. Sensation of swelling in pharynx, with stiffness of neck, and head drawn to one side ; diphtheria. Dryness of throat ; afterward soreness. Stomach.- (Lachnanthes) Aversion to meat. (Arn., Carb. v., Graph., Puls.) Abdomen.- (Lachnanthes) Continuous rumbling and rolling in abdomen, as of gas. (Aloe, Lyc.) Stool and Anus.- (Lachnanthes) Frequent stool ; passes much wind. Respiratory Organs.- (Lachnanthes) Cough dry as if from larynx, with sore throat. Stitches in chest under right breast and under the clavicle. Heat in chest. (Acon., Ars., Sang.) Heart and Pulse.- (Lachnanthes) Pulse slow, irregular. (Digit.) Sensation of heat in region of heart. Neck and Back.- (Lachnanthes) Stiffness of the neck. (Rhus tox.) Pain in nape of neck as if from dislocation. Burning in the sacral and lumbar region. Generalities.- (Lachnanthes) Very restless ; throws herself about (Acon., Ars.) ; feels weak. (Cinch., Coccul.) Skin.- (Lachnanthes) Pimples appear here and there, containing a watery fluid. (Graph.) Stinging, itching, and tingling on thigh, legs, feet, arms, etc. (Acon., Apis, Urt. ur.) Sleep.- (Lachnanthes) Sleepless , or restless sleep, with distressing dreams. Cannot go to sleep easily. Fever.- (Lachnanthes) Flashes of heat alternating with chilliness. Burning in palms of hands and soles of feet. (Sulph.) Heat, with redness of face ; after heat circumscribed redness of both cheeks ; worse on right side. (Sang.) Perspires freely, especially after midnight. During cold sensation the skin is moist and sticky. Therapeutic Range.- (Lachnanthes) Diphtheria ; cerebro-spinal meningitis ; wry neck. Compare.- (Lachnanthes) Agar., Bell., Cic., Cimic., Camph., Hyos., Lach., Stram. Copyright © Robert Séror 2003

KEY-NOTES TO THE MATERIA MEDICA by HENRY N. GUERNSEY, M.D. Presented by Médi-T LACHNANTHES We think of this remedy where the neck is drawn over to one side from diphtheria; sore throat; rheumatism in the neck, etc. Torticollis . Copyright © Médi-T 2000

_raw: Compare: Aletris, the Irises and Veratrums (botan.); heat in heart (Croc., Op., Rho.); pain in lower right lung (Chel., K. ca., Sep.─right apex, Ars., Calc.; left apex, Ars.; left base, Oxal. ac., Sul.; right middle lobe, Sep.; right chest, Sang., Phell. aq., Zn.); cold between shoulders (Am. m.;─heat between shoulders, Lyc., Pho.); loquacity (Lach., Hyo.); brilliant eyes, red cheeks, delirium (Bell.). Compare also: Act. r., Æth., Agar., Can. ind., Cic., Crotal., Sele., Glo., Gymnoc., Plat., Stram.

mental_emotional.text: Restlessness, tosses about (during perspiration). Great loquacity; afterwards stupid and irritable. Ill-humoured and sleepy. Great hilarity (evening). Became much excited over a trifle. Loquacious delirium, with brilliant eyes and circumscribed red cheeks (1 to 2 a.m.).

head.text: Giddiness in the head, with sensation of heat in chest and around heart, and perspiration. Giddiness with icy coldness of the forehead. Painful tearing in forehead in the open air. Dull headache and pressing in forehead. Tearing in forehead from l. to r. side. On forehead elevated red pimples. Wrinkles on forehead, with longitudinal ridges from interior corner of eyebrows upward. Head feels heavy. Sensation as if the vertex were enlarged and were driven upward. Tearing in vertex. Headache pressing eyes outward. The head feels enlarged, and as if split open with a wedge from the outside to within; the body is icy cold; she cannot get warm also for a long time under a feather bed; the whole face becomes yellow; she has to whine with the headache; the head burns like fire, with much thirst; during the cold sensation the skin is moist and clammy. Tearing in (r.) temple, extending to cheek. Pricking headache (evening). Sensation as if the hair were standing on end; in the occiput. Scalp very painful to touch.

eyes.text: Obscuration of sight; could not see in evening; as if a cloud were before the eyes. Pressing in eyes as from dust; with secretion of white mucus. Secretion of white mucus from eyes (canthi). Twitching of r. canthus. Sensation of twitching in upper eyelids, when closing them. In morning, violent lachrymation and burning of eyes, with sensation of dryness. The eyes feel heavy, as if they could not be kept open. Eyes dry. Eyes feel cold. The eyebrows and upper eyelids are drawn upwards; looks with fixed eyes. Yellow spots before eyes. Bright, sparkling eyes with delirium.

ears.text: Singing in ear when walking in open air. Tearing in ears. Crawling and itching in ears. Itching or tingling in ears; > by boring with the finger, but immediately returning and feeling as if something had closed ear. Almost complete deafness (during fever).

nose.text: The nose bleeds profusely; the blood is pale. Bridge of nose feels as if pinched.

face.text: Face swollen, with redness and blueness under eyes. Pale, sickly countenance; face and lips bluish; eyes dull, feel thick and cold. Circumscribed redness of face (1 a.m. to 8 am.), with violent delirium and brilliant eyes. Redness of face; yellow face. Sensation as if something were crawling over face.

mouth_throat.text: Pain in all the teeth when eating or drinking warm things. All the teeth feel loose and too long; in bed. Saliva of tough mucus. Swelling and tension of the lips. Lips red. Sensation as if the mouth were sore and thick. Great dryness in throat, esp. on awaking during the night, with much coughing. Sore throat, with short cough. When swallowing, itching (in a small spot). Sensation of swelling in pharynx, with stiffness of neck, and head drawn to one side (diphtheria).

stomach_abdomen.text: Aversion to meat. Much thirst. Sensation of qualmishness above navel, when walking in open air. In pit of stomach, beating as from a pulse, as if a hammer were beating on an ulcerated spot. Fulness in pit of stomach. Rolling of wind in abdomen, and discharge of flatulency. Sensation of heat through abdomen.

stool_rectum.text: Frequent stool; passes much wind. Frequent unsuccessful desire to evacuate. Continuous stitch in rectum.

male.text: Violent burning in l. half of the scrotum, drawing towards r. side. Tingling and itching of scrotum. Perspiration and itching of scrotum and penis.

female.text: Menses too early, blood viscid, mixed with mucus. During menses, sensation of distension of abdomen; it feels as if it were boiling. Menstrual blood profuse, bright red.

chest_heart.text: Bubbling and boiling in chest and region of heart; it rises to the head and he becomes giddy; he breaks out into a perspiration. Sensation of heat in region of heart. Stitches in heart, with anxiety. Frequent violent pulsations of heart, each beat is double, one hard and full, the other soft and small. Trembling of heart with great debility.

back.text: Stiffness and pain in neck, extending over whole head down to the nose, and sensation as if nostrils were pinched together. Sensation of spraining in neck when turning or moving head backward. Stiff neck; the head is drawn to one side (after diphtheria, scarlet fever. Burning on r. scapula. Sensation as if a piece of ice were lying on back, between the shoulders, followed by a chill, with gooseflesh all over. Sensation between the shoulder-blades as if wet with cold perspiration, the skin being dry and cool. Burning in region of l. kidney, extending towards r. side. Burning in spine above sacrum. Burning in sacrum.

skin.text: Sensation in skin as if an eruption would appear. Pimples appear here and there, containing a watery fluid. The red pimples on the forehead suppurate. Itching and burning in the skin all night; after scratching. Stinging, itching, and tingling on thighs, legs, feet, arms, &c.

sleep.text: Sleepiness with yawning, eyes feel so heavy that she cannot keep them open. Sleepless, or restless sleep, with distressing dreams. Cannot go to sleep easily. After waking and stretching, a shock followed by chilliness and gooseflesh all over.

fever_chill.text: Flashes of heat alternating with chilliness. Continuous chilliness. Sensation as if a piece of ice were lying on back between shoulders, then a shock, followed by coldness over whole body with gooseflesh; these attacks recur on moving and go off after going to bed. Feels hot, but the chills run all over her before the heat can develop itself. Icy coldness of body. Heat with redness of face; after heat circumscribed redness of both cheeks; on r. side; fever with violent delirium and brilliant eyes ( from 1 a.m. to 2 a.m.). Fever with somnolency. Perspires freely (after restless sleep), esp. after midnight. The skin is cold, damp and clammy during the coldness. Icy cold perspiration, principally on forehead. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

indications.text: The whole plant Lachnanthes tinctoria, Elliott.Nat. Ord.—Haemodoraceae.COMMON NAMES: Red root, Spirit weed.

Botanical Source.—This is a perennial plant, introduced into practice by the Homoeopaths. It has a red fibrous root, and an erect stem, strict, 18 to 24 inches high, clothed with white wool above. The leaves are mostly radical, fleshy, equitant, sword-shaped, 3 to 4 inches wide, and nearly as high as the stem; the cauline leaves remote and bract-like. The corymb is terminal, close, 15 to 30-flowered; the flowers densely clothed with white wool outside, glabrous and yellow within. Perianth woolly outside, 6-parted down to the adherent ovary. Calyx lobes exterior, of 3 linear sepals, as long as the 3 lance-oblong petals. Stamens 3, opposite the sepals; filaments long, exserted; anthers linear, bright-yellow. Style thread-like, exserted, declined; stigma minutely 2-lobed. Capsule globular, truncated, 3-celled, many-seeded; seeds few on each fleshy placenta, flat and rounded, fixed by the middle (W.—G.).

History.—This plant is a native of the United States, growing in sandy swamps and along borders of ponds, near the Atlantic coast, from Rhode Island to Florida, flowering in July. The root has been used for dyeing purposes, and, according to Dr. Byron, has been used among the Florida Indians to produce a brilliancy of the eye, a flushed and swollen face, a bold appearance, and eloquent speaking; after these peculiar stimulating effects pass off, the person becomes stupid and very irritable. The method of employing it is to make the whole plant into a saturated tincture.

Action, Medical Uses, and Dosage.—Large doses of lachnanthes produce dilatation of the pupils, impaired vision, dizziness, and other unpleasant symptoms, somewhat similar to those produced by belladonna. Lachnanthes has been more particularly recommended in pneumonia, nervous and typhus fevers, some diseases of the brain, in the delirium of fever, in morbid conditions of the brain and nervous system, especially when in these several maladies redness of the cheeks and brilliancy of the eyes are accompanying symptoms. It has also been efficient in rheumatic wry neck, hoarseness, laryngeal cough, tinnitus aurium, and in nervous headache. A fluid drachm of the tincture added to 4 fluid ounces of water, and administered in fluid drachm doses, every 3 or 4 hours, is the proper method of administering it.
King's American Dispensatory, 1898, was written by Harvey Wickes Felter, M.D., and John Uri Lloyd, Phr. M., Ph. D.
